title: Extract Data from Contracts: Legal Automation for Contract Management
metaTitle: Extract Data from Contracts - Legal Automation | PdfExcel.ai
metaDescription: Convert contract PDFs to structured Excel spreadsheets. Extract key terms, dates, and obligations with AI-powered legal automation.
h1: Extract Data from Contracts with Legal Automation

hero.headline: Turn Contract PDFs into Structured Excel Spreadsheets
hero.subheadline: Extract key contract terms, dates, and obligations into organized spreadsheets for better legal document management and compliance tracking.

summary: Legal teams can extract data from contracts by converting PDF contract files into structured Excel spreadsheets using AI-powered automation. This process transforms unstructured contract documents into organized data tables containing key terms, dates, party information, and obligations that can be easily tracked and analyzed.
whenThisIsRelevant: Due diligence processes requiring contract analysis
Compliance audits needing structured contract data
supportedInputs: Contract images in PNG or JPEG format
expectedOutputs: Excel spreadsheets with contract data fields
CSV files with structured contract information
commonChallenges: Manual contract review taking weeks per document
Missing key dates and renewal deadlines
Inconsistent contract data formatting across documents
Difficulty tracking obligations across large contract portfolios
howItWorksSteps: Upload contract PDF files or scanned documents
Select specific contract fields to extract (dates, parties, terms, amounts)
AI processes documents and extracts structured data
Download organized Excel spreadsheet with contract information
whyPdfExcelAiFits: AI-powered extraction handles various contract formats and layouts
Custom field selection focuses on relevant contract terms
Batch processing handles multiple contracts simultaneously
OCR technology processes both digital and scanned contract documents
limitations: Complex multi-page contract tables may require manual review
Heavily redacted contracts will have missing data fields
Handwritten contract amendments have limited recognition accuracy

faq.question: What contract information can be extracted into Excel?
faq.answer: Key contract data including party names, effective dates, expiration dates, payment terms, renewal clauses, termination conditions, and specific obligations can be extracted into structured spreadsheet columns.

faq.question: Can the system process scanned contract documents?
faq.answer: Yes, OCR technology processes scanned PDF contracts and contract images, though accuracy depends on document clarity and quality of the original scan.

faq.question: How accurate is contract data extraction?
faq.answer: Accuracy exceeds 99% on clear, well-formatted contracts but may be lower on complex layouts, heavily redacted documents, or poor-quality scans requiring manual verification.

faq.question: Can multiple contracts be processed simultaneously?
faq.answer: Yes, batch processing allows multiple contract files to be uploaded and processed together, with results organized in a single Excel file with one row per contract.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and automatically deleted after processing
Supports both digital and scanned contract formats
Pay-as-you-go pricing with free trial available
exampleUseCases: Law firms extracting key terms from client contracts for case preparation
Corporate legal departments tracking contract renewal dates across vendor agreements
Real estate companies organizing lease terms and conditions in spreadsheet format
Insurance companies extracting policy details from contract documents for compliance reporting
